Params: {
    "textDocument": {
        "uri": "file:///Volumes/repos/stance-2/packages/stance-schema/src/effectiveness-history-crud.ts",
        "languageId": "typescript",
        "version": 1,
        "text": "import { getDrizzle, type DrizzleTransactionClient } from \"@nonfx/utils/drizzle-client\";\nimport { buildAncestorMap } from \"@nonfx/utils\";\nimport { eq, and, gte, inArray, isNull } from \"drizzle-orm\";\nimport {\n\teffectivenessHistoryTable,\n\teffectivenessSnapshotsTable,\n\ttype DBEffectivenessHistoryInsertSchema,\n\ttype DBEffectivenessHistorySchema,\n\ttype DBEffectivenessSnapshotSchema,\n\ttype SnapshotTagEffectiveness,\n\ttype SnapshotCategoryEffectiveness\n} from \"./effectiveness-history\";\nimport { applicationStancesTable, applicationStatementsTable } from \"./applications\";\nimport { stanceDocumentsTable } from \"./stances\";\nimport { documentsTable } from \"./documents/documents-schema\";\nimport { statementsTable } from \"./documents/statements-schema\";\nimport { statementClauseJunctionTable, clauseStarmapJunctionTable } from \"./clauses\";\nimport {\n\tfetchAllStarmapsWithoutFields,\n\tfetchFullStarmapFromId,\n\ttype StarmapField\n} from \"./starmap\";\n\n/** Supported time range presets */\nexport const EFFECTIVENESS_HISTORY_RANGES = [\"7d\", \"30d\", \"90d\", \"1y\"] as const;\nexport type EffectivenessHistoryRange = (typeof EFFECTIVENESS_HISTORY_RANGES)[number];\n\n/** Compute a Date cutoff for a given range */\nfunction getRangeCutoff(range: EffectivenessHistoryRange): Date {\n\tconst now = new Date();\n\tswitch (range) {\n\t\tcase \"7d\":\n\t\t\treturn new Date(now.getTime() - 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n\t\tcase \"30d\":\n\t\t\treturn new Date(now.getTime() - 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n\t\tcase \"90d\":\n\t\t\treturn new Date(now.getTime() - 90 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n\t\tcase \"1y\":\n\t\t\treturn new Date(now.getTime() - 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tthrow new Error(`Unsupported EffectivenessHistoryRange: ${range satisfies never}`);\n\t}\n}\n\n/** Result of fetchRmfContext — maps statements to starmap fields/tags/categories */\nexport type RmfContext = {\n\t/** Map: statementId → Set of starmapFieldIds (direct mappings via clauses) */\n\tstatementToFieldIds: Map<string, Set<string>>;\n\t/** All RMF starmap fields indexed by ID */\n\trmfFieldById: Map<string, Omit<StarmapField, \"embedding\">>;\n\t/** Root category fields (indent === 0) */\n\trootCategoryFields: Array<Omit<StarmapField, \"embedding\">>;\n\t/** Map: fieldId → Set of ancestor fieldIds */\n\tancestorMap: Map<string, Set<string>>;\n};\n\n/**\n * Build RMF context: map statement IDs to starmap fields, tags, and categories.\n * Returns null if no RMF starmap exists.\n */\nexport async function fetchRmfContext(\n\tstatementIds: Array<string>,\n\ttx?: DrizzleTransactionClient\n): Promise<RmfContext | null> {\n\tconst db = tx ?? getDrizzle();\n\n\t// 1. Find the RMF starmap\n\tconst allStarmaps = await fetchAllStarmapsWithoutFields();\n\tconst rmfStarmap = allStarmaps.find(s => s.metadataType === \"rmf\");\n\tif (!rmfStarmap) {\n\t\treturn null;\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Get full fields\n\tconst fullStarmap = await fetchFullStarmapFromId(rmfStarmap.id);\n\tconst { fields } = fullStarmap;\n\n\t// 3. Build fieldById lookup\n\tconst rmfFieldById = new Map<string, Omit<StarmapField, \"embedding\">>();\n\tfor (const f of fields) {\n\t\trmfFieldById.set(f.id, f);\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Query statementClauseJunction JOIN clauseStarmapJunction\n\t//    to build Map<statementId, Set<starmapFieldId>>\n\tif (statementIds.length === 0) {\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\tstatementToFieldIds: new Map(),\n\t\t\trmfFieldById,\n\t\t\trootCategoryFields: fields.filter(f => f.indent === 0),\n\t\t\tancestorMap: buildAncestorMap(fields)\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\n\tconst junctionRows = await db\n\t\t.select({\n\t\t\tstatementId: statementClauseJunctionTable.statementId,\n\t\t\tstarmapFieldId: clauseStarmapJunctionTable.starmapFieldId\n\t\t})\n\t\t.from(statementClauseJunctionTable)\n\t\t.innerJoin(\n\t\t\tclauseStarmapJunctionTable,\n\t\t\tand(\n\t\t\t\teq(clauseStarmapJunctionTable.clauseId, statementClauseJunctionTable.clauseId),\n\t\t\t\teq(clauseStarmapJunctionTable.starmapId, rmfStarmap.id)\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t)\n\t\t.where(inArray(statementClauseJunctionTable.statementId, statementIds));\n\n\tconst statementToFieldIds = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n\tfor (const row of junctionRows) {\n\t\tif (!statementToFieldIds.has(row.statementId)) {\n\t\t\tstatementToFieldIds.set(row.statementId, new Set());\n\t\t}\n\t\tstatementToFieldIds.get(row.statementId)!.add(row.starmapFieldId);\n\t}\n\n\t// 5. Build ancestor map\n\tconst ancestorMap = buildAncestorMap(fields);\n\n\treturn {\n\t\tstatementToFieldIds,\n\t\trmfFieldById,\n\t\trootCategoryFields: fields.filter(f => f.indent === 0),\n\t\tancestorMap\n\t};\n}\n\n/**\n * Insert a single effectiveness change event.\n */\nexport async function insertEffectivenessEvent(\n\tdata: Omit<DBEffectivenessHistoryInsertSchema, \"id\" | \"createdAt\" | \"updatedAt\">,\n\ttx?: DrizzleTransactionClient\n) {\n\tconst db = tx ?? getDrizzle();\n\tconst rows = await db.insert(effectivenessHistoryTable).values(data).returning();\n\treturn rows[0]!;\n}\n\n/**\n * Compute and store both stance-level AND per-app effectiveness snapshots.\n *\n * Stance-level logic (from dashboard.get.ts):\n * - effective: ALL apps have status \"effective\"\n * - notTested: ANY app has null status\n * - ineffective: otherwise (at least one ineffective, all tested)\n *\n * Per-app logic:\n * - Each app's own counts for its statements (effective/ineffective/notTested)\n *\n * Returns the stance-level snapshot.\n */\nexport async function computeAndStoreSnapshot(\n\tstanceId: string,\n\ttriggeredBy: string,\n\ttriggerApplicationId: string,\n\ttriggerStatementId: string,\n\ttx?: DrizzleTransactionClient\n): Promise<DBEffectivenessSnapshotSchema> {\n\tconst db = tx ?? getDrizzle();\n\n\t// 1. Get all apps in this stance\n\tconst appsInStance = await db\n\t\t.select({ applicationId: applicationStancesTable.applicationId })\n\t\t.from(applicationStancesTable)\n\t\t.where(eq(applicationStancesTable.stanceId, stanceId));\n\n\tconst appIds = appsInStance.map(a => a.applicationId);\n\n\tif (appIds.length === 0) {\n\t\t// No apps -> empty stance-level snapshot (applicationId = null)\n\t\tconst rows = await db\n\t\t\t.insert(effectivenessSnapshotsTable)\n\t\t\t.values({\n\t\t\t\tstanceId,\n\t\t\t\tapplicationId: null,\n\t\t\t\ttriggeredBy,\n\t\t\t\ttriggerApplicationId,\n\t\t\t\ttriggerStatementId,\n\t\t\t\ttotalControls: 0,\n\t\t\t\teffectiveControls: 0,\n\t\t\t\tineffectiveControls: 0,\n\t\t\t\tnotTestedControls: 0,\n\t\t\t\teffectivenessPercentage: \"0.00\",\n\t\t\t\ttagEffectiveness: null,\n\t\t\t\tcategoryEffectiveness: null\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t.returning();\n\t\treturn rows[0]!;\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Get all application-statement records for these apps\n\tconst allAppStatements = await db\n\t\t.select({\n\t\t\tapplicationId: applicationStatementsTable.applicationId,\n\t\t\tstatementId: applicationStatementsTable.statementId,\n\t\t\tstatus: applicationStatementsTable.status\n\t\t})\n\t\t.from(applicationStatementsTable)\n\t\t.where(inArray(applicationStatementsTable.applicationId, appIds));\n\n\t// 2b. Get the full set of stance-level statements via stanceDocuments → documents → statements.\n\t// This captures statements that may not be linked to all apps (e.g. ENABLE_CLASSIFICATION_SERVICE_CONTROL).\n\tconst stanceStatementRows = await db\n\t\t.selectDistinct({ statementId: statementsTable.id })\n\t\t.from(stanceDocumentsTable)\n\t\t.innerJoin(documentsTable, eq(documentsTable.name, stanceDocumentsTable.documentName))\n\t\t.innerJoin(statementsTable, eq(statementsTable.documentId, documentsTable.id))\n\t\t.where(eq(stanceDocumentsTable.stanceId, stanceId));\n\n\t// Build the full statement set: union of stance-level + any from applicationStatements\n\tconst allStatementIds = new Set<string>(stanceStatementRows.map(r => r.statementId));\n\tfor (const record of allAppStatements) {\n\t\tallStatementIds.add(record.statementId);\n\t}\n\n\t// Build a lookup: (appId:statementId) → status, for quick gap detection\n\tconst appStmtStatusMap = new Map<string, string | null>();\n\tfor (const record of allAppStatements) {\n\t\tappStmtStatusMap.set(`${record.applicationId}:${record.statementId}`, record.status);\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. Build per-statement effectiveness across apps (for stance-level)\n\t// Each statement is expected across ALL apps in the stance (appIds.length).\n\tconst controlEffectiveness = new Map<\n\t\tstring,\n\t\t{ totalApps: number; effectiveApps: number; ineffectiveApps: number; notTestedApps: number }\n\t>();\n\n\t// Also build per-app counts\n\tconst perAppCounts = new Map<\n\t\tstring,\n\t\t{ total: number; effective: number; ineffective: number; notTested: number }\n\t>();\n\n\t// Initialize per-app counts\n\tfor (const appId of appIds) {\n\t\tperAppCounts.set(appId, { total: 0, effective: 0, ineffective: 0, notTested: 0 });\n\t}\n\n\t// Iterate over every (statement, app) pair to account for missing mappings\n\tfor (const statementId of allStatementIds) {\n\t\tcontrolEffectiveness.set(statementId, {\n\t\t\ttotalApps: appIds.length,\n\t\t\teffectiveApps: 0,\n\t\t\tineffectiveApps: 0,\n\t\t\tnotTestedApps: 0\n\t\t});\n\t\tconst eff = controlEffectiveness.get(statementId)!;\n\n\t\tfor (const appId of appIds) {\n\t\t\tconst key = `${appId}:${statementId}`;\n\t\t\tconst status = appStmtStatusMap.has(key) ? appStmtStatusMap.get(key) : undefined;\n\n\t\t\tconst appCount = perAppCounts.get(appId)!;\n\t\t\tappCount.total += 1;\n\n\t\t\tif (status === undefined) {\n\t\t\t\t// Missing (app, statement) pair — treat as notTested\n\t\t\t\teff.notTestedApps += 1;\n\t\t\t\tappCount.notTested += 1;\n\t\t\t} else if (status === \"effective\") {\n\t\t\t\teff.effectiveApps += 1;\n\t\t\t\tappCount.effective += 1;\n\t\t\t} else if (status === \"ineffective\") {\n\t\t\t\teff.ineffectiveApps += 1;\n\t\t\t\tappCount.ineffective += 1;\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// null status = notTested\n\t\t\t\teff.notTestedApps += 1;\n\t\t\t\tappCount.notTested += 1;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Compute stance-level counts (same logic as dashboard.get.ts:505-536)\n\tlet totalControls = 0;\n\tlet effectiveControls = 0;\n\tlet ineffectiveControls = 0;\n\tlet notTestedControls = 0;\n\n\tfor (const [, eff] of controlEffectiveness) {\n\t\tif (eff.totalApps === 0) {\n\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t}\n\t\ttotalControls += 1;\n\n\t\tconst isStanceEffective = eff.totalApps > 0 && eff.effectiveApps === eff.totalApps;\n\t\tconst hasNotTestedApps = eff.notTestedApps > 0;\n\t\tconst isStanceIneffective = !isStanceEffective && !hasNotTestedApps && eff.ineffectiveApps > 0;\n\n\t\tif (isStanceEffective) {\n\t\t\teffectiveControls += 1;\n\t\t} else if (hasNotTestedApps) {\n\t\t\tnotTestedControls += 1;\n\t\t} else if (isStanceIneffective) {\n\t\t\tineffectiveControls += 1;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnotTestedControls += 1;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute stance-level percentage as average of per-app percentages\n\t// (instead of strict \"all apps must pass\" which gives 0% if any app is untested)\n\tconst appPercentages = appIds.map(appId => {\n\t\tconst counts = perAppCounts.get(appId)!;\n\t\treturn counts.total > 0 ? (counts.effective / counts.total) * 100 : 0;\n\t});\n\tconst percentage =\n\t\tappPercentages.length > 0\n\t\t\t? (appPercentages.reduce((sum, p) => sum + p, 0) / appPercentages.length).toFixed(2)\n\t\t\t: \"0.00\";\n\n\tconst snapshotAt = new Date().toISOString();\n\n\t// 5. Compute per-tag and per-category effectiveness via RMF context\n\tlet tagEffectiveness: Array<SnapshotTagEffectiveness> | null = null;\n\tlet categoryEffectiveness: Array<SnapshotCategoryEffectiveness> | null = null;\n\n\tconst rmfCtx = await fetchRmfContext(Array.from(allStatementIds), tx);\n\tif (rmfCtx) {\n\t\t// Pre-compute: statementId → Set<tagName> (expand via ancestors to collect all tags)\n\t\tconst stmtToTags = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n\t\t// Also collect tag metadata: tagName → { color, icon }\n\t\tconst tagMeta = new Map<string, { color: string; icon: string }>();\n\t\t// Pre-compute: statementId → Set<rootCategoryFieldId> (which root categories does this stmt roll up to)\n\t\tconst stmtToCategories = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n\n\t\tfor (const statementId of allStatementIds) {\n\t\t\tconst directFieldIds = rmfCtx.statementToFieldIds.get(statementId);\n\t\t\tif (!directFieldIds || directFieldIds.size === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Expand field IDs + their ancestors\n\t\t\tconst allFieldIds = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\tfor (const fid of directFieldIds) {\n\t\t\t\tallFieldIds.add(fid);\n\t\t\t\tconst ancestors = rmfCtx.ancestorMap.get(fid);\n\t\t\t\tif (ancestors) {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const aid of ancestors) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallFieldIds.add(aid);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Collect tags from all expanded fields\n\t\t\tconst tags = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\tfor (const fid of allFieldIds) {\n\t\t\t\tconst field = rmfCtx.rmfFieldById.get(fid);\n\t\t\t\tif (field?.tags) {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const t of field.tags) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttags.add(t.name);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!tagMeta.has(t.name)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttagMeta.set(t.name, { color: t.color ?? \"#888888\", icon: t.icon ?? \"tag\" });\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (tags.size > 0) {\n\t\t\t\tstmtToTags.set(statementId, tags);\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Find which root categories this statement rolls up to\n\t\t\tconst categories = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\tfor (const fid of allFieldIds) {\n\t\t\t\tconst field = rmfCtx.rmfFieldById.get(fid);\n\t\t\t\tif (field && field.indent === 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcategories.add(field.id);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (categories.size > 0) {\n\t\t\t\tstmtToCategories.set(statementId, categories);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// --- Per-tag effectiveness (average of per-app percentages) ---\n\t\tconst allTagNames = new Set<string>();\n\t\tfor (const tags of stmtToTags.values()) {\n\t\t\tfor (const t of tags) {\n\t\t\t\tallTagNames.add(t);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif (allTagNames.size > 0) {\n\t\t\tconst tagResults: Array<SnapshotTagEffectiveness> = [];\n\t\t\tfor (const tagName of allTagNames) {\n\t\t\t\t// Statements that have this tag\n\t\t\t\tconst stmtsWithTag = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\t\tfor (const [stmtId, tags] of stmtToTags) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (tags.has(tagName)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tstmtsWithTag.add(stmtId);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// For each app: count controls with this tag, count effective ones\n\t\t\t\tconst appPcts: Array<number> = [];\n\t\t\t\tfor (const appId of appIds) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet tagTotal = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tlet tagEffective = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const stmtId of stmtsWithTag) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst key = `${appId}:${stmtId}`;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst status = appStmtStatusMap.has(key) ? appStmtStatusMap.get(key) : undefined;\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttagTotal += 1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (status === \"effective\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttagEffective += 1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif (tagTotal > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tappPcts.push((tagEffective / tagTotal) * 100);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tconst avgPct = appPcts.length > 0 ? appPcts.reduce((s, p) => s + p, 0) / appPcts.length : 0;\n\t\t\t\tconst controlsInTag = stmtsWithTag.size;\n\t\t\t\tconst meta = tagMeta.get(tagName)!;\n\t\t\t\ttagResults.push({\n\t\t\t\t\ttagName,\n\t\t\t\t\ttagColor: meta.color,\n\t\t\t\t\ttagIcon: meta.icon,\n\t\t\t\t\tcontrolsInTag,\n\t\t\t\t\tcontrolsEffectiveInTag: Math.round((avgPct * controlsInTag) / 100)\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttagEffectiveness = tagResults;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// --- Per-category effectiveness (average of per-app percentages) ---\n\t\tif (rmfCtx.rootCategoryFields.length > 0) {\n\t\t\tconst catResults: Array<SnapshotCategoryEffectiveness> = [];\n\t\t\tfor (const catField of rmfCtx.rootCategoryFields) {\n\t\t\t\t// Statements rolling up to this category\n\t\t\t\tconst stmtsInCat = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\t\tfor (const [stmtId, cats] of stmtToCategories) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (cats.has(catField.id)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tstmtsInCat.add(stmtId);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif (stmtsInCat.size === 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// For each app: count controls in this category, count effective ones\n\t\t\t\tconst appPcts: Array<number> = [];\n\t\t\t\tfor (const appId of appIds) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet catTotal = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tlet catEffective = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const stmtId of stmtsInCat) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst key = `${appId}:${stmtId}`;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst status = appStmtStatusMap.has(key) ? appStmtStatusMap.get(key) : undefined;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcatTotal += 1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (status === \"effective\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcatEffective += 1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif (catTotal > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tappPcts.push((catEffective / catTotal) * 100);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tconst avgPct = appPcts.length > 0 ? appPcts.reduce((s, p) => s + p, 0) / appPcts.length : 0;\n\t\t\t\tconst controlsInCategory = stmtsInCat.size;\n\t\t\t\tcatResults.push({\n\t\t\t\t\tcategoryId: catField.id,\n\t\t\t\t\tcategoryName: catField.title,\n\t\t\t\t\tcategoryTitle: catField.fullTitle,\n\t\t\t\t\tcontrolsInCategory,\n\t\t\t\t\tcontrolsEffectiveInCategory: Math.round((avgPct * controlsInCategory) / 100)\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcategoryEffectiveness = catResults.length > 0 ? catResults : null;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// 6. Store stance-level snapshot (applicationId = null)\n\tconst stanceSnapshotValues = {\n\t\tstanceId,\n\t\tapplicationId: null as string | null,\n\t\ttriggeredBy,\n\t\ttriggerApplicationId,\n\t\ttriggerStatementId,\n\t\ttotalControls,\n\t\teffectiveControls,\n\t\tineffectiveControls,\n\t\tnotTestedControls,\n\t\teffectivenessPercentage: percentage,\n\t\ttagEffectiveness,\n\t\tcategoryEffectiveness,\n\t\tsnapshotAt\n\t};\n\n\t// 7. Build per-app snapshot values\n\tconst perAppSnapshotValues = appIds.map(appId => {\n\t\tconst counts = perAppCounts.get(appId)!;\n\t\tconst appPct = counts.total > 0 ? ((counts.effective / counts.total) * 100).toFixed(2) : \"0.00\";\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\tstanceId,\n\t\t\tapplicationId: appId,\n\t\t\ttriggeredBy,\n\t\t\ttriggerApplicationId,\n\t\t\ttriggerStatementId,\n\t\t\ttotalControls: counts.total,\n\t\t\teffectiveControls: counts.effective,\n\t\t\tineffectiveControls: counts.ineffective,\n\t\t\tnotTestedControls: counts.notTested,\n\t\t\teffectivenessPercentage: appPct,\n\t\t\ttagEffectiveness: null as Array<SnapshotTagEffectiveness> | null,\n\t\t\tcategoryEffectiveness: null as Array<SnapshotCategoryEffectiveness> | null,\n\t\t\tsnapshotAt\n\t\t};\n\t});\n\n\t// 8. Insert all snapshots (stance-level + per-app) in one batch\n\tconst allSnapshotValues = [stanceSnapshotValues, ...perAppSnapshotValues];\n\tconst rows = await db.insert(effectivenessSnapshotsTable).values(allSnapshotValues).returning();\n\n\t// Return the stance-level snapshot (first row, applicationId = null)\n\treturn rows[0]!;\n}\n\n/**\n * Query effectiveness snapshots for a stance within a time range.\n * When applicationId is provided, returns per-app snapshots for that app.\n * When applicationId is omitted, returns stance-level snapshots (applicationId IS NULL).\n * Returns rows ordered by snapshotAt ascending (oldest first) for charting.\n *\n * For ranges > 7d, deduplicates to one snapshot per day (keeps last per day)\n * to reduce payload size for long time ranges.\n */\nexport async function getEffectivenessSnapshots(\n\tstanceId: string,\n\trange: EffectivenessHistoryRange,\n\tapplicationId?: string,\n\ttx?: DrizzleTransactionClient\n): Promise<Array<DBEffectivenessSnapshotSchema>> {\n\tconst db = tx ?? getDrizzle();\n\tconst cutoff = getRangeCutoff(range);\n\n\tconst conditions = [\n\t\teq(effectivenessSnapshotsTable.stanceId, stanceId),\n\t\tgte(effectivenessSnapshotsTable.snapshotAt, cutoff.toISOString())\n\t];\n\n\tif (applicationId) {\n\t\tconditions.push(eq(effectivenessSnapshotsTable.applicationId, applicationId));\n\t} else {\n\t\tconditions.push(isNull(effectivenessSnapshotsTable.applicationId));\n\t}\n\n\tconst rows = await db\n\t\t.select()\n\t\t.from(effectivenessSnapshotsTable)\n\t\t.where(and(...conditions))\n\t\t.orderBy(effectivenessSnapshotsTable.snapshotAt);\n\n\t// Quantize: for long ranges (90d, 1y), keep only the last snapshot per calendar day\n\t// to reduce payload size. For 7d/30d the data volume is manageable and we preserve\n\t// all points for smooth chart rendering.\n\tif ((range === \"90d\" || range === \"1y\") && rows.length > 0) {\n\t\tconst byDay = new Map<string, DBEffectivenessSnapshotSchema>();\n\t\tfor (const row of rows) {\n\t\t\tconst day = row.snapshotAt.substring(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD\n\t\t\tbyDay.set(day, row); // last per day wins (rows are sorted asc)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn Array.from(byDay.values());\n\t}\n\n\treturn rows;\n}\n\n/**\n * Query effectiveness change events for a stance within a time range.\n * Returns rows ordered by changedAt ascending.\n */\nexport async function getEffectivenessEvents(\n\tstanceId: string,\n\trange: EffectivenessHistoryRange,\n\ttx?: DrizzleTransactionClient\n): Promise<Array<DBEffectivenessHistorySchema>> {\n\tconst db = tx ?? getDrizzle();\n\tconst cutoff = getRangeCutoff(range);\n\n\treturn await db\n\t\t.select()\n\t\t.from(effectivenessHistoryTable)\n\t\t.where(\n\t\t\tand(\n\t\t\t\teq(effectivenessHistoryTable.stanceId, stanceId),\n\t\t\t\tgte(effectivenessHistoryTable.changedAt, cutoff.toISOString())\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t)\n\t\t.orderBy(effectivenessHistoryTable.changedAt);\n}\n"
    }

starmapFieldsCache.get(meta.starmapFieldId);\n\t\tconst starmap = starmapsCache.get(meta.metadataId);\n\n\t\tif (field && starmap) {\n\t\t\t// Format as \"MetadataName > FieldTitle\" (e.g., \"Phase > Plan\")\n\t\t\tlines.push(`${starmap.metadataName} > ${field.fullTitle}`);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn lines.join(\"\\n\");\n}\n\ntype ProcessClauseMatchesOptions = {\n\tclauseId: string;\n\tleftStatementId: string; // NEW: needed to look up statement ranks\n\tcolumnDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>;\n\tcorrelationLookup: Map<string, Array<{ id: string; statement_id: string }>>;\n\tstatementRanksLookup: Map<string, Map<string, number>>; // NEW: statement-level ranks\n\tcolumnClausesCache: Map<\n\t\tstring,\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tid: string;\n\t\t\tfullActivity: string;\n\t\t\tstatementId: string;\n\t\t\tstatementTitle: string;\n\t\t\tstatementDisplayId: string;\n\t\t}\n\t>;\n\tstatementToDocumentMap: Map<string, string>;\n\t/** Statement ID → full lineage string for right-side (column) statements */\n\tcolumnLineageMap: Map<string, string>;\n};\n\n/**\n * Process matches for a single clause and group by document\n * Uses statement-level ranks from parallel ranking structure\n */\nfunction processClauseMatches(opts: ProcessClauseMatchesOptions): Map<string, Array<MatchInfo>> {\n\tconst {\n\t\tclauseId,\n\t\tleftStatementId,\n\t\tcolumnDocuments,\n\t\tcorrelationLookup,\n\t\tstatementRanksLookup,\n\t\tcolumnClausesCache,\n\t\tstatementToDocumentMap,\n\t\tcolumnLineageMap\n\t} = opts;\n\n\tconst matchesByDocument = new Map<string, Array<MatchInfo>>();\n\n\t// Initialize empty arrays for each column document\n\tfor (const colDoc of columnDocuments) {\n\t\tmatchesByDocument.set(colDoc.id, []);\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up matches for this clause from correlation\n\t// Matches are already sorted by similarity and have rank values from correlation handler\n\tconst matchingClauses = correlationLookup.get(clauseId) ?? [];\n\n\t// Get statement ranks for this left statement\n\tconst statementRanks = statementRanksLookup.get(leftStatementId) ?? new Map();\n\n\t// Process this clause's matching clauses\n\tfor (const matchingClause of matchingClauses) {\n\t\tconst matchedDocId = statementToDocumentMap.get(matchingClause.statement_id);\n\t\tif (!matchedDocId || !matchesByDocument.has(matchedDocId)) {\n\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconst matches = matchesByDocument.get(matchedDocId)!;\n\t\t// Avoid duplicates (same statement + same clause)\n\t\tif (\n\t\t\t!matches.some(\n\t\t\t\tm =>\n\t\t\t\t\tm.statementId === matchingClause.statement_id && m.matchedClauseId === matchingClause.id\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t) {\n\t\t\tconst clauseInfo = columnClausesCache.get(matchingClause.id);\n\t\t\t// Get statement::statement rank from parallel structure\n\t\t\tconst rank = statementRanks.get(matchingClause.statement_id) ?? 0;\n\t\t\tmatches.push({\n\t\t\t\tstatementId: matchingClause.statement_id,\n\t\t\t\tdisplayId: clauseInfo?.statementDisplayId ?? \"-\",\n\t\t\t\ttext: columnLineageMap.get(matchingClause.statement_id) ?? clauseInfo?.statementTitle ?? \"\",\n\t\t\t\trank,\n\t\t\t\tmatchedClauseId: matchingClause.id,\n\t\t\t\tmatchedClauseText: clauseInfo?.fullActivity ?? \"\"\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort matches by rank (highest first) within each document\n\tfor (const [docId, matches] of matchesByDocument.entries()) {\n\t\tmatches.sort((a, b) => b.rank - a.rank);\n\t\tmatchesByDocument.set(docId, matches);\n\t}\n\n\treturn matchesByDocument;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build report rows from FULL document data (not just correlation results)\n * This ensures ALL clauses are shown, including unmatched ones\n * Each row represents ONE clause with its matches\n * @param includeRightClauses - When false, matched clause text is not included in output (only statement text). Does not affect number of matches shown.\n */\nexport function buildReportRows(\n\tdata: CustomReportData,\n\tincludeRightClauses = true\n): Array<ReportRow> {\n\tconst {\n\t\trowDocuments,\n\t\tcolumnDocuments,\n\t\tcorrelation,\n\t\tstarmapFieldsCache,\n\t\tstarmapsCache,\n\t\tselectedMetadataIds\n\t} = data;\n\tconst rows: Array<ReportRow> = [];\n\n\t// Build helper maps from full documents\n\tconst statementToDocumentMap = buildStatementToDocumentMap(columnDocuments);\n\tconst correlationLookup = buildCorrelationLookup(correlation);\n\tconst statementRanksLookup = buildStatementRanksLookup(correlation);\n\tconst columnClausesCache = buildColumnClausesCache(columnDocuments);\n\n\t// Lineage maps: statement ID → \"Ancestor 1 > Ancestor 2 > Title\"\n\tconst rowLineageMap = buildStatementLineageMap(rowDocuments);\n\tconst columnLineageMap = buildStatementLineageMap(columnDocuments);\n\n\t// Iterate over ALL statements/clauses in row documents (not just correlated ones)\n\tfor (const rowDoc of rowDocuments) {\n\t\tfor (const statement of rowDoc.statements) {\n\t\t\t// If statement has no clauses (nodes), create one row with empty clause info\n\t\t\tif (statement.nodes.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tconst emptyMatchByDocument = new Map<string, MatchInfo | null>();\n\t\t\t\tfor (const colDoc of columnDocuments) {\n\t\t\t\t\temptyMatchByDocument.set(colDoc.id, null);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\trows.push({\n\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementId: statement.id,\n\t\t\t\t\tleftDisplayId: statement.displayId ?? \"-\",\n\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementText: rowLineageMap.get(statement.id) ?? statement.title ?? \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseId: \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseText: \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseStarmaps: \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseAttributes: \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchByDocument: emptyMatchByDocument,\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchIndex: 1,\n\t\t\t\t\ttotalMatches: 0\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Process each clause (node) from the full document\n\t\t\tfor (const clauseNode of statement.nodes) {\n\t\t\t\t// Always use full clause-to-clause matching to get all matches\n\t\t\t\t// The includeRightClauses flag only affects whether we show clause text, not how many matches we show\n\t\t\t\tlet matchesByDocument = processClauseMatches({\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseId: clauseNode.id,\n\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementId: statement.id,\n\t\t\t\t\tcolumnDocuments,\n\t\t\t\t\tcorrelationLookup,\n\t\t\t\t\tstatementRanksLookup,\n\t\t\t\t\tcolumnClausesCache,\n\t\t\t\t\tstatementToDocumentMap,\n\t\t\t\t\tcolumnLineageMap\n\t\t\t\t});\n\n\t\t\t\t// When not including right clauses, deduplicate matches by statement ID\n\t\t\t\t// (keep only the best match per unique statement)\n\t\t\t\tif (!includeRightClauses) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst dedupedMatches = new Map<string, Array<MatchInfo>>();\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const [docId, matches] of matchesByDocument.entries()) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst seenStatements = new Map<string, MatchInfo>();\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor (const match of matches) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tconst existing = seenStatements.get(match.statementId);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Keep the match with the higher rank\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!existing || match.rank > existing.rank) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tseenStatements.set(match.statementId, match);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdedupedMatches.set(docId, Array.from(seenStatements.values()));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchesByDocument = dedupedMatches;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Find max matches across all column documents\n\t\t\t\tconst matchArrays = Array.from(matchesByDocument.values());\n\t\t\t\tconst totalMatches = Math.max(...matchArrays.map(arr => arr.length), 0);\n\n\t\t\t\t// If no matches for this clause, still create one row (shows unmatched clauses)\n\t\t\t\tconst displayMatches = totalMatches === 0 ? 1 : totalMatches;\n\n\t\t\t\t// Format starmap metadata for this clause (only selected ones from correlation config)\n\t\t\t\tconst clauseStarmaps = formatClauseStarmaps(\n\t\t\t\t\tclauseNode.metadatas,\n\t\t\t\t\tstarmapFieldsCache,\n\t\t\t\t\tstarmapsCache,\n\t\t\t\t\tselectedMetadataIds\n\t\t\t\t);\n\n\t\t\t\t// Create one row per match index for this clause\n\t\t\t\tfor (let i = 0; i < displayMatches; i++) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst matchByDocument = new Map<string, MatchInfo | null>();\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const colDoc of columnDocuments) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst matches = matchesByDocument.get(colDoc.id) ?? [];\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchByDocument.set(colDoc.id, matches[i] ?? null);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\trows.push({\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementId: statement.id,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftDisplayId: statement.displayId ?? \"-\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementText: rowLineageMap.get(statement.id) ?? statement.title ?? \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseId: clauseNode.id,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseText: clauseNode.fullActivity,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseStarmaps,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseAttributes: formatClauseAttributes(clauseNode.attributesV1 ?? null),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchByDocument,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchIndex: i + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttotalMatches\n\t\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn rows;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create Overview sheet with summary information\n */\nfunction createOverviewSheet(\n\tworkbook: ExcelJS.Workbook,\n\tdata: CustomReportData,\n\treportRows: Array<ReportRow>\n): void {\n\tconst worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet(\"Overview\");\n\tworksheet.views = [{ showGridLines: false }];\n\n\t// Title\n\tworksheet.mergeCells(\"B2:G3\");\n\tconst titleCell = worksheet.getCell(\"B2\");\n\ttitleCell.value = \"Custom Report\";\n\ttitleCell.font = { size: 24, bold: true, color: { argb: COLORS.darkText } };\n\ttitleCell.alignment = { horizontal: \"center\", vertical: \"middle\" };\n\ttitleCell.fill = { type: \"pattern\", pattern: \"solid\", fgColor: { argb: COLORS.white } };\n\n\t// Generated date\n\tworksheet.getCell(\"B5\").value = \"Generated:\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(\"B5\").font = { bold: true, color: { argb: COLORS.mutedText } };\n\tworksheet.getCell(\"C5\").value = new Date().toLocaleString();\n\n\t// Row Documents section\n\tlet currentRow = 8;\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).value = \"Row Documents (Left Side):\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).font = {\n\t\tbold: true,\n\t\tsize: 12,\n\t\tcolor: { argb: COLORS.darkText }\n\t};\n\tcurrentRow++;\n\n\tfor (const doc of data.rowDocuments) {\n\t\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).value = doc.displayName ?? doc.name;\n\t\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).font = { color: { argb: COLORS.darkText } };\n\t\tcurrentRow++;\n\t}\n\n\tcurrentRow += 2;\n\n\t// Column Documents section\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).value = \"Column Documents (Right Side):\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).font = {\n\t\tbold: true,\n\t\tsize: 12,\n\t\tcolor: { argb: COLORS.darkText }\n\t};\n\tcurrentRow++;\n\n\tfor (const doc of data.columnDocuments) {\n\t\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).value = doc.displayName ?? doc.name;\n\t\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).font = { color: { argb: COLORS.darkText } };\n\t\tcurrentRow++;\n\t}\n\n\tcurrentRow += 2;\n\n\t// Statistics\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).value = \"Statistics:\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`B${currentRow}`).font = {\n\t\tbold: true,\n\t\tsize: 12,\n\t\tcolor: { argb: COLORS.darkText }\n\t};\n\tcurrentRow++;\n\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).value = \"Total Rows:\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).font = { bold: true, color: { argb: COLORS.mutedText } };\n\tworksheet.getCell(`D${currentRow}`).value = reportRows.length;\n\tcurrentRow++;\n\n\t// Count matches\n\tlet matchedRows = 0;\n\tfor (const row of reportRows) {\n\t\tconst hasMatch = Array.from(row.matchByDocument.values()).some(m => m !== null);\n\t\tif (hasMatch) {\n\t\t\tmatchedRows++;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).value = \"Rows with Matches:\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).font = { bold: true, color: { argb: COLORS.mutedText } };\n\tworksheet.getCell(`D${currentRow}`).value = matchedRows;\n\tcurrentRow++;\n\n\tconst matchPercentage =\n\t\treportRows.length > 0 ? Math.round((matchedRows / reportRows.length) * 100) : 0;\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).value = \"Match Percentage:\";\n\tworksheet.getCell(`C${currentRow}`).font = { bold: true, color: { argb: COLORS.mutedText } };\n\tworksheet.getCell(`D${currentRow}`).value = `${matchPercentage}%`;\n\n\t// Set column widths\n\tworksheet.getColumn(2).width = 30;\n\tworksheet.getColumn(3).width = 25;\n\tworksheet.getColumn(4).width = 15;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a string to be used as an Excel worksheet name\n * Removes invalid characters: * ? : \\ / [ ]\n */\nfunction sanitizeWorksheetName(name: string): string {\n\treturn name.replace(/[*?:\\\\/[\\]]/g, \"-\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Create Data sheet with the matrix of rows vs columns\n * Sheet is named after the row (source) document\n */\nfunction createDataSheet(\n\tworkbook: ExcelJS.Workbook,\n\tdata: CustomReportData,\n\treportRows: Array<ReportRow>,\n\tincludeRightClauses: boolean\n): void {\n\t// Use the first (and only) row document's name as the sheet name\n\tconst [rowDoc] = data.rowDocuments;\n\tconst rawName = rowDoc?.displayName ?? rowDoc?.name ?? \"Data\";\n\tconst sheetName = sanitizeWorksheetName(rawName).substring(0, 31); // Excel limits sheet names to 31 chars\n\tconst worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet(sheetName);\n\tworksheet.views = [{ state: \"frozen\", xSplit: 4, ySplit: 1 }];\n\n\t// Check if AI ranking was used (any row has aiScore)\n\tconst hasAiRanking = reportRows.some(row => {\n\t\tfor (const match of row.matchByDocument.values()) {\n\t\t\tif (match?.aiScore !== undefined) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false;\n\t});\n\n\t// Build headers: Display ID, Statement, Clause Text, Clause Metadata, then for each column doc: Statement [+ Matched Clause (if included)] + Rank [+ AI Score + AI Reasoning]\n\tconst headers: Array<string> = [\"Display ID\", \"Statement\", \"Clause Text\", \"Clause Metadata\"];\n\tfor (const colDoc of data.columnDocuments) {\n\t\tconst colName = colDoc.displayName ?? colDoc.name;\n\t\theaders.push(`${colName} Statement`);\n\t\tif (includeRightClauses) {\n\t\t\theaders.push(`${colName} Matched Clause`);\n\t\t}\n\t\theaders.push(`${colName} Rank`);\n\t\tif (hasAiRanking) {\n\t\t\theaders.push(`${colName} AI Score`);\n\t\t\theaders.push(`${colName} AI Reasoning`);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconst headerRow = worksheet.getRow(1);\n\theaderRow.values = headers;\n\tapplyHeaderStyle(headerRow, headers.length);\n\n\t// Build statement ID to color index map (for row coloring by statement)\n\tconst statementColorMap = new Map<string, number>();\n\tlet colorIndex = 0;\n\tfor (const row of reportRows) {\n\t\tif (!statementColorMap.has(row.leftStatementId)) {\n\t\t\tstatementColorMap.set(row.leftStatementId, colorIndex);\n\t\t\tcolorIndex++;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Data rows\n\treportRows.forEach((row, index) => {\n\t\tconst dataRow = worksheet.getRow(index + 2);\n\n\t\t// Build clause metadata cell: starmaps first, then \"Attributes\" header, then attributes\n\t\tlet clauseMetadata = \"\";\n\t\tif (row.clauseStarmaps) {\n\t\t\tclauseMetadata = row.clauseStarmaps;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (row.clauseAttributes) {\n\t\t\tif (clauseMetadata) {\n\t\t\t\tclauseMetadata += \"\\n\\nAttributes\\n\";\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tclauseMetadata = \"Attributes\\n\";\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tclauseMetadata += row.clauseAttributes;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Build row values - use string | number for rank values\n\t\tconst values: Array<string | number> = [\n\t\t\trow.leftDisplayId,\n\t\t\trow.leftStatementText,\n\t\t\trow.clauseText,\n\t\t\tclauseMetadata\n\t\t];\n\n\t\tfor (const colDoc of data.columnDocuments) {\n\t\t\tconst match = row.matchByDocument.get(colDoc.id);\n\t\t\tif (match) {\n\t\t\t\tvalues.push(match.text);\n\t\t\t\tif (includeRightClauses) {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(match.matchedClauseText || \"—\");\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvalues.push(match.rank > 0 ? match.rank : \"—\");\n\t\t\t\tif (hasAiRanking) {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(match.aiScore ?? \"—\");\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(match.aiReasoning ?? \"—\");\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tvalues.push(\"—\");\n\t\t\t\tif (includeRightClauses) {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(\"—\");\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvalues.push(\"—\");\n\t\t\t\tif (hasAiRanking) {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(\"—\");\n\t\t\t\t\tvalues.push(\"—\");\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdataRow.values = values;\n\t\t// Color rows by statement group, not by individual row\n\t\tconst statementColorIndex = statementColorMap.get(row.leftStatementId) ?? 0;\n\t\tapplyAlternatingRowStyle(dataRow, statementColorIndex, headers.length);\n\t});\n\n\t// Set column widths\n\tworksheet.getColumn(1).width = 12; // Display ID\n\tworksheet.getColumn(2).width = 50; // Statement\n\tworksheet.getColumn(3).width = 50; // Clause Text\n\tworksheet.getColumn(4).width = 40; // Clause Attributes\n\n\t// For each column doc, set widths for Statement [+ Matched Clause (if included)] + Rank [+ AI Score + AI Reasoning]\n\tlet colIndex = 5;\n\tfor (const _colDoc of data.columnDocuments) {\n\t\tworksheet.getColumn(colIndex).width = 40; // Statement\n\t\tcolIndex++;\n\n\t\tif (includeRightClauses) {\n\t\t\tworksheet.getColumn(colIndex).width = 50; // Matched Clause\n\t\t\tcolIndex++;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tworksheet.getColumn(colIndex).width = 10; // Rank\n\t\tcolIndex++;\n\n\t\tif (hasAiRanking) {\n\t\t\tworksheet.getColumn(colIndex).width = 12; // AI Score\n\t\t\tworksheet.getColumn(colIndex + 1).width = 60; // AI Reasoning\n\t\t\tcolIndex += 2;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add autofilter\n\tconst lastColumnLetter = getExcelColumnLetter(headers.length);\n\tworksheet.autoFilter = {\n\t\tfrom: \"A1\",\n\t\tto: `${lastColumnLetter}${reportRows.length + 1}`\n\t};\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply fullMatchesOnly filter to report rows.\n * For columns with fullMatchesOnly enabled, nulls out matches where the\n * right-side statement doesn't have ALL of its clauses matched.\n */\nexport function applyFullMatchesFilter(\n\treportRows: Array<ReportRow>,\n\tcolumnDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>,\n\tcorrelation: Array<CorrelatedDocumentOutput>,\n\tfilterConfig: ColumnFilterConfig\n): Array<ReportRow> {\n\tif (filterConfig.fullMatchesOnly.size === 0) {\n\t\treturn reportRows;\n\t}\n\n\t// Build a map of right-side statementId -> total clause count\n\tconst rightStatementClauseCount = new Map<string, number>();\n\tfor (const doc of columnDocuments) {\n\t\tfor (const stmt of doc.statements) {\n\t\t\trightStatementClauseCount.set(stmt.id, stmt.nodes.length);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build a map of right-side statementId -> Set of matched clause IDs\n\tconst rightStatementMatchedClauses = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n\tfor (const doc of correlation) {\n\t\tfor (const stmt of doc.statements) {\n\t\t\tfor (const node of stmt.nodes) {\n\t\t\t\t// node.matchingClauses contains right-side clauses that matched this left-side clause\n\t\t\t\tfor (const matchedClause of node.matchingClauses) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst rightStmtId = matchedClause.statement_id;\n\t\t\t\t\tconst rightClauseId = matchedClause.id;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!rightStatementMatchedClauses.has(rightStmtId)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\trightStatementMatchedClauses.set(rightStmtId, new Set());\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\trightStatementMatchedClauses.get(rightStmtId)!.add(rightClauseId);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build set of right-side statement IDs that have full matches (all clauses matched)\n\tconst fullyMatchedRightStatements = new Set<string>();\n\tfor (const [stmtId, matchedClauses] of rightStatementMatchedClauses.entries()) {\n\t\tconst totalClauses = rightStatementClauseCount.get(stmtId) ?? 0;\n\t\tif (totalClauses > 0 && matchedClauses.size >= totalClauses) {\n\t\t\tfullyMatchedRightStatements.add(stmtId);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter rows: for fullMatchesOnly columns, null out partial matches\n\treturn reportRows.map(row => {\n\t\tconst filteredMatchByDocument = new Map(row.matchByDocument);\n\t\tfor (const docId of filterConfig.fullMatchesOnly) {\n\t\t\tconst match = filteredMatchByDocument.get(docId);\n\t\t\tif (match && !fullyMatchedRightStatements.has(match.statementId)) {\n\t\t\t\t// This right-side statement doesn't have all its clauses matched - filter it out\n\t\t\t\tfilteredMatchByDocument.set(docId, null);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn { ...row, matchByDocument: filteredMatchByDocument };\n\t});\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply AI ranking to report rows.\n * For each clause with matches, score all matches using AI and add aiScore/aiReasoning fields.\n * @param includeRightClauses - When false, uses statement text for comparison instead of clause text\n */\nexport async function applyAiRanking(\n\treportRows: Array<ReportRow>,\n\tcolumnClausesCache: Map<\n\t\tstring,\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tid: string;\n\t\t\tfullActivity: string;\n\t\t\tstatementId: string;\n\t\t\tstatementTitle: string;\n\t\t\tstatementDisplayId: string;\n\t\t}\n\t>,\n\tgetPromptConfig: PromptFunctionOptions[\"getPromptConfig\"],\n\tincludeRightClauses = true\n): Promise<Array<ReportRow>> {\n\t// Create a queue with concurrency of 10 to limit parallel AI calls\n\tconst queue = new PQueue({ concurrency: 10 });\n\n\t// Group rows by clauseId to avoid scoring the same clause multiple times\n\tconst rowsByClause = new Map<string, Array<ReportRow>>();\n\tfor (const row of reportRows) {\n\t\tif (!row.clauseId) {\n\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t} // Skip rows without clauses\n\n\t\tif (!rowsByClause.has(row.clauseId)) {\n\t\t\trowsByClause.set(row.clauseId, []);\n\t\t}\n\t\trowsByClause.get(row.clauseId)!.push(row);\n\t}\n\n\t// Process each clause's matches\n\tconst scoredRowsByClause = new Map<string, Array<ReportRow>>();\n\n\tawait queue.addAll(\n\t\tArray.from(rowsByClause.entries()).map(([clauseId, clauseRows]) => async () => {\n\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\t// Get the first row to extract clause info and all document IDs\n\t\t\t\tconst firstRow = clauseRows[0];\n\t\t\t\tif (!firstRow) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Get all document IDs from the first row (includes all column documents)\n\t\t\t\tconst allDocIds = Array.from(firstRow.matchByDocument.keys());\n\n\t\t\t\t// Collect all unique matches across all rows for this clause (up to 100)\n\t\t\t\tconst matchesByDocId = new Map<string, Array<MatchInfo>>();\n\n\t\t\t\t// Initialize empty arrays for all documents\n\t\t\t\tfor (const docId of allDocIds) {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchesByDocId.set(docId, []);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor (const row of clauseRows) {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const [docId, match] of row.matchByDocument.entries()) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!match) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Avoid duplicates\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst matches = matchesByDocId.get(docId)!;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!matches.some(m => m.matchedClauseId === match.matchedClauseId)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches.push(match);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Truncate to first 100 matches total\n\t\t\t\tconst allMatches = Array.from(matchesByDocId.values()).flat();\n\t\t\t\tconst truncatedMatches = allMatches.slice(0, 100);\n\n\t\t\t\t// If no matches, skip AI scoring\n\t\t\t\tif (truncatedMatches.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tscoredRowsByClause.set(clauseId, clauseRows);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Prepare matches for AI scoring\n\t\t\t\tconst matchesForAi = truncatedMatches.map((match, index) => ({\n\t\t\t\t\tindex,\n\t\t\t\t\ttext: match.matchedClauseText || match.text\n\t\t\t\t}));\n\n\t\t\t\t// Call AI to score matches\n\t\t\t\tconst scoringResult = await scoreMatches(\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftClause: firstRow.clauseText,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches: matchesForAi\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t{ getPromptConfig }\n\t\t\t\t);\n\n\t\t\t\t// If AI scoring failed, keep original rows\n\t\t\t\tif (!scoringResult || !scoringResult.scores) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.warn(`AI scoring failed for clause ${clauseId}`, { firstRow });\n\t\t\t\t\tscoredRowsByClause.set(clauseId, clauseRows);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Build a map of matchedClauseId -> AI score/reasoning\n\t\t\t\tconst aiScoresByClauseId = new Map<string, { score: number; reasoning: string }>();\n\t\t\t\tfor (const scoreObj of scoringResult.scores) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst match = truncatedMatches[scoreObj.index];\n\t\t\t\t\tif (match) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\taiScoresByClauseId.set(match.matchedClauseId, {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tscore: scoreObj.score,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treasoning: scoreObj.reasoning\n\t\t\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Update matches with AI scores and sort by score within each document\n\t\t\t\tconst matchesByDocIdWithScores = new Map<string, Array<MatchInfo>>();\n\n\t\t\t\tfor (const docId of allDocIds) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst matches = matchesByDocId.get(docId) ?? [];\n\n\t\t\t\t\tconst scoredMatches = matches.map(match => {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst aiScore = aiScoresByClauseId.get(match.matchedClauseId);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (aiScore) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t...match,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\taiScore: aiScore.score,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\taiReasoning: aiScore.reasoning\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn match;\n\t\t\t\t\t});\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Sort by AI score descending (higher scores first)\n\t\t\t\t\tscoredMatches.sort((a, b) => {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst scoreA = a.aiScore ?? -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst scoreB = b.aiScore ?? -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn scoreB - scoreA;\n\t\t\t\t\t});\n\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchesByDocIdWithScores.set(docId, scoredMatches);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Find max matches across all column documents for this clause\n\t\t\t\tconst maxMatches = Math.max(\n\t\t\t\t\t...Array.from(matchesByDocIdWithScores.values()).map(arr => arr.length),\n\t\t\t\t\t0\n\t\t\t\t);\n\n\t\t\t\t// Rebuild rows with sorted matches\n\t\t\t\tconst rebuiltRows: Array<ReportRow> = [];\n\t\t\t\tconst displayMatches = maxMatches === 0 ? 1 : maxMatches;\n\n\t\t\t\tfor (let i = 0; i < displayMatches; i++) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst matchByDocument = new Map<string, MatchInfo | null>();\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Ensure all documents are represented\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const docId of allDocIds) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst matches = matchesByDocIdWithScores.get(docId) ?? [];\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchByDocument.set(docId, matches[i] ?? null);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\trebuiltRows.push({\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementId: firstRow.leftStatementId,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftDisplayId: firstRow.leftDisplayId,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tleftStatementText: firstRow.leftStatementText,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseId: firstRow.clauseId,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseText: firstRow.clauseText,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseStarmaps: firstRow.clauseStarmaps,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tclauseAttributes: firstRow.clauseAttributes,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchByDocument,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchIndex: i + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttotalMatches: maxMatches\n\t\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tscoredRowsByClause.set(clauseId, rebuiltRows);\n\t\t\t} catch (error) {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.error(`Error scoring clause ${clauseId}:`, error);\n\t\t\t\t// On error, keep original rows\n\t\t\t\tscoredRowsByClause.set(clauseId, clauseRows);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t);\n\n\t// Reconstruct the full rows array preserving left-side ordering\n\t// We iterate through original rows to preserve document/statement order,\n\t// but output the rebuilt (AI-sorted) rows for each clause\n\tconst result: Array<ReportRow> = [];\n\tconst processedClauseIds = new Set<string>();\n\n\tfor (const row of reportRows) {\n\t\tif (!row.clauseId) {\n\t\t\t// Rows without clauses - keep as is\n\t\t\tresult.push(row);\n\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip if we've already processed this clause\n\t\tif (processedClauseIds.has(row.clauseId)) {\n\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Mark this clause as processed\n\t\tprocessedClauseIds.add(row.clauseId);\n\n\t\t// Get the rebuilt rows for this clause (with AI-sorted matches)\n\t\tconst scoredRows = scoredRowsByClause.get(row.clauseId);\n\t\tif (scoredRows) {\n\t\t\t// Output all rebuilt rows for this clause in their new AI-sorted order\n\t\t\tresult.push(...scoredRows);\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fallback: if scoring failed, output original rows for this clause\n\t\t\tconst originalClauseRows = rowsByClause.get(row.clauseId);\n\t\t\tif (originalClauseRows) {\n\t\t\t\tresult.push(...originalClauseRows);\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tresult.push(row);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate Excel report for custom report\n */\nexport async function generateCustomExcelReport(\n\tdata: CustomReportData,\n\tfilterConfig?: ColumnFilterConfig,\n\tgetPromptConfig?: PromptFunctionOptions[\"getPromptConfig\"]\n): Promise<Buffer> {\n\tif (filterConfig?.reportType === \"statement\") {\n\t\treturn generateStatementExcelReport(data, filterConfig, getPromptConfig);\n\t}\n\n\t// Existing clause report path unchanged\n\t// Build all report rows (no limit)\n\t// includeRightClauses defaults to true for backwards compatibility\n\tlet reportRows = buildReportRows(data, filterConfig?.includeRightClauses ?? true);\n\n\t// Filter by selected statement IDs if provided\n\tif (filterConfig?.selectedStatementIds && filterConfig.selectedStatementIds.size > 0) {\n\t\treportRows = reportRows.filter(row =>\n\t\t\tfilterConfig.selectedStatementIds!.has(row.leftStatementId)\n\t\t);\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply fullMatchesOnly filter if configured\n\tif (filterConfig) {\n\t\treportRows = applyFullMatchesFilter(\n\t\t\treportRows,\n\t\t\tdata.columnDocuments,\n\t\t\tdata.correlation,\n\t\t\tfilterConfig\n\t\t);\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter out empty rows if requested\n\tif (filterConfig?.hideEmptyRows) {\n\t\treportRows = reportRows.filter(row => {\n\t\t\t// Check if any column has a match\n\t\t\tfor (const match of row.matchByDocument.values()) {\n\t\t\t\tif (match !== null) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn false;\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply AI ranking if requested\n\tif (filterConfig?.useAiRanking && getPromptConfig) {\n\t\tlogger.info(\"Applying AI ranking to matches\");\n\t\t// Build column clauses cache for AI ranking\n\t\tconst columnClausesCache = buildColumnClausesCache(data.columnDocuments);\n\t\treportRows = await applyAiRanking(\n\t\t\treportRows,\n\t\t\tcolumnClausesCache,\n\t\t\tgetPromptConfig,\n\t\t\tfilterConfig.includeRightClauses ?? true\n\t\t);\n\t\tlogger.info(\"AI ranking completed\");\n\t}\n\n\t// Create workbook\n\tconst workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();\n\tworkbook.creator = \"TakeStance Custom Report Generator\";\n\tworkbook.created = new Date();\n\n\t// Create sheets\n\tcreateOverviewSheet(workbook, data, reportRows);\n\tcreateDataSheet(workbook, data, reportRows, filterConfig?.includeRightClauses ?? true);\n\n\t// Generate buffer\n\tconst buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();\n\treturn Buffer.from(buffer as ArrayBuffer);\n}\n\n/**\n * Statement report path: build statement rows → filter → AI rerank → XLSX\n */\nasync function generateStatementExcelReport(\n\tdata: CustomReportData,\n\tfilterConfig: ColumnFilterConfig,\n\tgetPromptConfig?: PromptFunctionOptions[\"getPromptConfig\"]\n): Promise<Buffer> {\n\tlet statementRows = buildStatementReportRows(data);\n\n\t// Filter by selected statement IDs if provided\n\tif (filterConfig.selectedStatementIds && filterConfig.selectedStatementIds.size > 0) {\n\t\tstatementRows = statementRows.filter(row =>\n\t\t\tfilterConfig.selectedStatementIds!.has(row.leftStatementId)\n\t\t);\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter out empty rows if requested\n\tif (filterConfig.hideEmptyRows) {\n\t\tstatementRows = statementRows.filter(row => {\n\t\t\tfor (const matches of row.matchesByDocument.values()) {\n\t\t\t\tif (matches.length > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn false;\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply AI reranking if requested — reranks the full candidate pool and\n\t// truncates to AI_RERANK_TOP_MATCHES_COUNT inside applyStatementAiReranking\n\tif (filterConfig.useAiRanking && getPromptConfig) {\n\t\tlogger.info(\"Applying AI reranking to statement matches\");\n\t\tstatementRows = await applyStatementAiReranking(\n\t\t\tstatementRows,\n\t\t\tdata.rowDocuments,\n\t\t\tdata.columnDocuments,\n\t\t\tgetPromptConfig\n\t\t);\n\t\tlogger.info(\"AI reranking completed\");\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Without AI reranking, still truncate to top N by embedding rank\n\t\tfor (const row of statementRows) {\n\t\t\tfor (const [docId, matches] of row.matchesByDocument.entries()) {\n\t\t\t\tif (matches.length > AI_RERANK_TOP_MATCHES_COUNT) {\n\t\t\t\t\trow.matchesByDocument.set(docId, matches.slice(0, AI_RERANK_TOP_MATCHES_COUNT));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create workbook\n\tconst workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();\n\tworkbook.creator = \"TakeStance Custom Report Generator\";\n\tworkbook.created = new Date();\n\n\t// Create sheets - reuse overview with a dummy clause-report-row count\n\tconst overviewRows: Array<ReportRow> = statementRows.map(r => ({\n\t\tleftStatementId: r.leftStatementId,\n\t\tleftDisplayId: r.leftDisplayId,\n\t\tleftStatementText: r.leftStatementText,\n\t\tclauseId: \"\",\n\t\tclauseText: \"\",\n\t\tclauseStarmaps: \"\",\n\t\tclauseAttributes: \"\",\n\t\tmatchByDocument: new Map(),\n\t\tmatchIndex: 1,\n\t\ttotalMatches: 0\n\t}));\n\tcreateOverviewSheet(workbook, data, overviewRows);\n\tcreateStatementDataSheet(workbook, data, statementRows);\n\n\t// Generate buffer\n\tconst buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();\n\treturn Buffer.from(buffer as ArrayBuffer);\n}\n\n// Starmap field info for lookup\ntype StarmapFieldInfo = {\n\tid: string;\n\ttitle: string;\n\tfullTitle: string;\n\tstarmapId: string;\n};\n\n// Starmap (metadata) info for lookup\ntype StarmapInfo = {\n\tid: string;\n\tmetadataName: string;\n};\n\nexport type CustomReportData = {\n\t// Full row documents with all statements and clauses\n\trowDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>;\n\t// Full column documents with all statements and clauses\n\tcolumnDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>;\n\t// Correlation output - only contains matched statements/clauses with pre-calculated ranks\n\tcorrelation: Array<CorrelatedDocumentOutput>;\n\t// Starmap field lookup: starmapFieldId -> field info\n\tstarmapFieldsCache: Map<string, StarmapFieldInfo>;\n\t// Starmaps lookup: starmapId -> starmap info\n\tstarmapsCache: Map<string, StarmapInfo>;\n\t// Selected metadata IDs from correlation configuration (only show these starmaps)\n\tselectedMetadataIds: Set<string>;\n};\n\n// Type for match info including matched clause data\nexport type MatchInfo = {\n\tstatementId: string;\n\tdisplayId: string;\n\ttext: string;\n\trank: number;\n\tmatchedClauseId: string;\n\tmatchedClauseText: string;\n\taiScore?: number;\n\taiReasoning?: string;\n};\n\n// Type for denormalized report row - each row represents ONE clause\nexport type ReportRow = {\n\tleftStatementId: string;\n\tleftDisplayId: string;\n\tleftStatementText: string;\n\t// Single clause for this row\n\tclauseId: string;\n\tclauseText: string;\n\t// Starmap metadata formatted as \"MetadataName > FieldTitle\" lines\n\tclauseStarmaps: string;\n\tclauseAttributes: string;\n\t// Map of column document ID -> statement data with rank and matched clause\n\tmatchByDocument: Map<string, MatchInfo | null>;\n\t// Match info: which match index this row represents for this clause\n\tmatchIndex: number;\n\ttotalMatches: number;\n};\n\n// Configuration for filtering columns\nexport type ColumnFilterConfig = {\n\t/** Document IDs where only full matches should be shown */\n\tfullMatchesOnly: Set<string>;\n\t/** When provided, only include rows for these statement IDs */\n\tselectedStatementIds?: Set<string>;\n\t/** When true, remove rows that have no matches in any column */\n\thideEmptyRows?: boolean;\n\t/** When true, use AI to score and rank matches */\n\tuseAiRanking?: boolean;\n\t/** When true, include matched clause text columns in the report. When false, only show statement text (default: true) */\n\tincludeRightClauses?: boolean;\n\t/** Report type: 'clause' for clause-level rows, 'statement' for statement-level rows */\n\treportType?: \"clause\" | \"statement\";\n};\n\n// ==========================================\n// Statement Report Types and Functions\n// ==========================================\n\nexport type StatementMatchInfo = {\n\tstatementId: string;\n\tdisplayId: string;\n\ttext: string;\n\trank: number;\n\taiTier?: \"headline\" | \"standard\";\n\taiReasoning?: string;\n};\n\nexport type StatementReportRow = {\n\tleftStatementId: string;\n\tleftDisplayId: string;\n\tleftStatementText: string;\n\tmatchesByDocument: Map<string, Array<StatementMatchInfo>>;\n};\n\n/**\n * Build statement-level report rows from full document data.\n * Statement reports deduplicate at statement level because users care about\n * which statements match, not which specific clauses caused the match.\n * Takes top AI_RERANK_CANDIDATE_POOL_SIZE candidates per column so the LLM\n * can rerank them down to AI_RERANK_TOP_MATCHES_COUNT.\n */\nexport function buildStatementReportRows(data: CustomReportData): Array<StatementReportRow> {\n\tconst { rowDocuments, columnDocuments, correlation } = data;\n\tconst rows: Array<StatementReportRow> = [];\n\n\tconst statementToDocumentMap = buildStatementToDocumentMap(columnDocuments);\n\tconst correlationLookup = buildCorrelationLookup(correlation);\n\tconst statementRanksLookup = buildStatementRanksLookup(correlation);\n\tconst columnClausesCache = buildColumnClausesCache(columnDocuments);\n\n\t// Lineage maps: statement ID → \"Ancestor 1 > Ancestor 2 > Title\"\n\tconst rowLineageMap = buildStatementLineageMap(rowDocuments);\n\tconst columnLineageMap = buildStatementLineageMap(columnDocuments);\n\n\tfor (const rowDoc of rowDocuments) {\n\t\tfor (const statement of rowDoc.statements) {\n\t\t\t// Collect all clause-level matches for this statement, grouped by column doc\n\t\t\tconst allMatchesByDoc = new Map<string, Map<string, StatementMatchInfo>>();\n\n\t\t\t// Initialize empty maps for each column document\n\t\t\tfor (const colDoc of columnDocuments) {\n\t\t\t\tallMatchesByDoc.set(colDoc.id, new Map());\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Get statement-level ranks for this left statement\n\t\t\tconst statementRanks = statementRanksLookup.get(statement.id) ?? new Map();\n\n\t\t\t// Process each clause in this statement\n\t\t\tfor (const clauseNode of statement.nodes) {\n\t\t\t\tconst matchingClauses = correlationLookup.get(clauseNode.id) ?? [];\n\n\t\t\t\tfor (const matchingClause of matchingClauses) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst matchedDocId = statementToDocumentMap.get(matchingClause.statement_id);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!matchedDocId || !allMatchesByDoc.has(matchedDocId)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tconst docMatches = allMatchesByDoc.get(matchedDocId)!;\n\t\t\t\t\tconst rightStatementId = matchingClause.statement_id;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Deduplicate by right-side statement ID, keep best rank\n\t\t\t\t\tconst existing = docMatches.get(rightStatementId);\n\t\t\t\t\tconst rank = statementRanks.get(rightStatementId) ?? 0;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!existing || rank > existing.rank) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst clauseInfo = columnClausesCache.get(matchingClause.id);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdocMatches.set(rightStatementId, {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tstatementId: rightStatementId,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdisplayId: clauseInfo?.statementDisplayId ?? \"-\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttext: columnLineageMap.get(rightStatementId) ?? clauseInfo?.statementTitle ?? \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\trank\n\t\t\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Convert to sorted arrays, take top N candidates for LLM reranking\n\t\t\tconst matchesByDocument = new Map<string, Array<StatementMatchInfo>>();\n\t\t\tfor (const [docId, matchesMap] of allMatchesByDoc.entries()) {\n\t\t\t\tconst sorted = Array.from(matchesMap.values()).sort((a, b) => b.rank - a.rank);\n\t\t\t\tmatchesByDocument.set(docId, sorted.slice(0, AI_RERANK_CANDIDATE_POOL_SIZE));\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\trows.push({\n\t\t\t\tleftStatementId: statement.id,\n\t\t\t\tleftDisplayId: statement.displayId ?? \"-\",\n\t\t\t\tleftStatementText: rowLineageMap.get(statement.id) ?? statement.title ?? \"\",\n\t\t\t\tmatchesByDocument\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn rows;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build parent lineage from rendered document to avoid extra DB queries.\n * The rendered document already has indent + title for all statements.\n */\nfunction buildParentLineageFromRenderedDoc(\n\tstatement: StanceRenderedDocument[\"statements\"][number],\n\tallStatements: StanceRenderedDocument[\"statements\"]\n): Array<string> {\n\tconst ancestors = findAncestorsOfElement(allStatements, statement).reverse();\n\treturn ancestors.map(ancestor =>\n\t\tgetStatementTitleWithParameterValue(ancestor.title, ancestor.parameters)\n\t);\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a map from statement ID to full lineage string (ancestors + self).\n * Uses rendered document's indent hierarchy so no DB queries are needed.\n */\nfunction buildStatementLineageMap(documents: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>): Map<string, string> {\n\tconst lineageMap = new Map<string, string>();\n\n\tfor (const doc of documents) {\n\t\tfor (const stmt of doc.statements) {\n\t\t\tconst ancestors = findAncestorsOfElement(doc.statements, stmt).reverse();\n\t\t\tconst parts = [\n\t\t\t\t...ancestors.map(a => getStatementTitleWithParameterValue(a.title, a.parameters)),\n\t\t\t\tgetStatementTitleWithParameterValue(stmt.title, stmt.parameters)\n\t\t\t];\n\t\t\tlineageMap.set(stmt.id, parts.join(\" > \"));\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn lineageMap;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply AI reranking to statement report rows.\n * For each row + each column doc with matches, calls rerankMatches() to produce\n * 2 tiers: headline (#1), standard (rest).\n */\nexport async function applyStatementAiReranking(\n\trows: Array<StatementReportRow>,\n\trowDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>,\n\tcolumnDocuments: Array<StanceRenderedDocument>,\n\tgetPromptConfig: PromptFunctionOptions[\"getPromptConfig\"]\n): Promise<Array<StatementReportRow>> {\n\tconst queue = new PQueue({ concurrency: 10 });\n\n\t// Build statement lookup maps for lineage\n\tconst
